tools.name: Kalshi
tools.description:
Real-time prediction market intelligence for Kalshi covering politics, economics, sports, climate, and financials with 23 tools for discovery, analysis, and cross-platform arbitrage detection.

Features:
- Discover trending markets ranked by volume, liquidity, or open interest with direct Kalshi URLs
- Analyze orderbook depth and simulate slippage for $100/$500/$1000 positions before trading
- Detect arbitrage opportunities where YES + NO prices sum below 100 cents for risk-free profit
- Cross-reference Kalshi markets against Polymarket to find pricing discrepancies across platforms
- Filter markets by probability tier (lottery tickets at 1-15% through near-certain at 85-95%)
- Historical candlestick data at 1-minute, hourly, and daily intervals for price trend analysis
- Full taxonomy browsing through categories, series, events, and individual markets with pagination
- Sentiment analysis combining price momentum, volume trends, and conviction scoring
- Market efficiency scoring that calculates vig, true implied probabilities, and exploitability ratings
- Slug resolution for Kalshi URLs so users can paste links directly for instant lookups
- API-native market filtering by status, event, series, category, timestamps, and ticker lists

Try asking:
- "What are the top 10 most actively traded markets on Kalshi right now?"
- "Show me all Kalshi categories and how many series each one has"
- "Find markets where YES is priced between 5 and 15 cents as lottery ticket plays"
- "Analyze the orderbook depth and slippage for KXBITCOIN and tell me if I can get $1000 in without moving the price"
- "Search for 'Fed rate cut' markets, check their efficiency, then compare the best one against Polymarket"
- "Are there any arbitrage opportunities in Politics markets where YES + NO is under 99 cents?"
- "What is the sentiment and price trend for the top Trump tariffs market over the last 24 hours?"
- "Get the daily candlestick chart for a Bitcoin price market over the past 30 days"
- "Browse the Sports category sorted by volume and cross-reference the top result with Polymarket odds"
- "Find all settled markets in the Economics category from the past month with their resolution prices"

Agent tips:
- Call discover_trending_markets or search_markets first to get valid tickers before using analysis tools
- Always use ticker values exactly as returned by the API; never modify or construct tickers manually
- For Kalshi URLs, extract the slug (first path segment after /markets/) and pass to get_event_by_slug
- Chain discovery workflows: get_all_categories -> get_all_series -> get_events -> get_market for full drill-down
- Use kalshi_crossref_polymarket to compare prices across platforms, but always verify resolution rules match before calculating arbitrage
- Pagination is supported on get_events, get_markets, and get_market_trades via the cursor parameter; pass the returned nextCursor to fetch subsequent pages
- Use get_markets with timestamp filters (minCloseTs, maxCloseTs) for time-scoped queries instead of filtering client-side

mcpTools.name: check_market_efficiency
mcpTools.description:
Check if a market is efficiently priced. Calculates the "vig" (YES + NO should = 100¢), identifies pricing inefficiencies.

For multi-outcome events, checks if all outcomes sum to 100%.

- Vig/overround percentage

mcpTools.name: kalshi_crossref_polymarket
mcpTools.description:
[KALSHI SERVER] Search Polymarket for markets equivalent to a Kalshi market.

This tool belongs to the KALSHI MCP server. Use it when you have a Kalshi market 
and want to find the corresponding market on Polymarket for comparison.

⚠️ Call this tool with KALSHI's toolId, not Polymarket's.

Uses Polymarket's official /public-search API for reliable text search.

WORKFLOW:
  1. You have a Kalshi market (from get_event_by_slug or search_markets)
  2. Call: kalshi_crossref_polymarket({ keywords: "supreme court trump tariffs" })
  3. Returns matching Polymarket markets with prices and rules

PRICE COMPARISON:
  - Polymarket: decimals (0.28 = 28%)
  - Kalshi: cents (28 = 28%)

⚠️ CRITICAL: Compare 'rules' and 'yesOutcomeMeans' fields!
Markets may define YES/NO differently - verify before calculating arbitrage.

title.description: The Kalshi market title to search for on Polymarket

keywords.description: Keywords to search (e.g., 'supreme court tariffs trump'). More specific = better results.

kalshiTicker.description: Optional: The Kalshi ticker (for reference in results)

comparisonNote.description: ⚠️ MUST READ: Step-by-step guide for comparing outcomes across platforms

slug.description: Use this with Polymarket's get_event_by_slug tool

noOutcomeMeans.description: ⚠️ CRITICAL: What does buying NO mean? Compare with Kalshi!

yesOutcomeMeans.description: ⚠️ CRITICAL: What does buying YES mean? Compare with Kalshi!

polymarketResults.description: Matching Polymarket markets. Use 'slug' with Polymarket's get_event_by_slug for details.

rateLimit.notes: Fan-out/heavy query path; prefer direct list endpoints when possible.

callOrderHints: Markets may define YES/NO differently - verify before calculating arbitrage
edgeCaseNotes: Use it when you have a Kalshi market and want to find the corresponding market on Polymarket for comparison

mcpTools.name: search_markets
mcpTools.description:
Search for Kalshi markets by keyword.

✅ ROBUST SEARCH: This tool searches Kalshi's /series endpoint (7,800+ series with titles)
   then fetches markets for matching series. This finds markets that don't appear in
   standard listings.

EXAMPLES:
  - "trump tariffs" → finds KXDJTVOSTARIFFS (Supreme Court tariffs case)
  - "supreme court" → finds all SCOTUS-related markets
  - "bitcoin" → finds all BTC price markets

IF YOU HAVE A KALSHI URL, use get_event_by_slug instead:
  - URL: https://kalshi.com/markets/kxdjtvostariffs/tariffs-case
  - Use: get_event_by_slug({ slug: "kxdjtvostariffs" })

⚠️ Use EXACT ticker values from results. NEVER modify tickers!
  - Correct: get_market({ ticker: "KXDJTVOSTARIFFS" })  ✅
  - Wrong: get_market({ ticker: "KXDJTVOSTARIFFS-001" })  ❌

STATUS OPTIONS:
  - 'open' (default): Active markets currently trading
  - 'settled': Resolved markets (for historical questions)
  - 'all': Both active and historical

tools.name: Polymarket
tools.description:
41-tool Polymarket MCP server for prediction market intelligence: whale cost analysis, market efficiency scoring, smart money flow tracking, arbitrage detection, portfolio analysis with context injection, and full read access to events, orderbooks, prices, trades, positions, and community data.

Features:
- Whale analytics: simulate exit cost for large positions (whale cost), track top holder concentration, analyze wallet-level flow patterns across events, and identify smart money movements
- Market efficiency and arbitrage: score markets for pricing inefficiency, detect cross-outcome arbitrage, find correlated markets, and surface high-conviction trading opportunities with configurable probability and volume filters
- Portfolio analysis with context injection: analyze your own Polymarket positions with exit liquidity simulation, P&L tracking, and personalized recommendations (uses contextRequirements for automatic portfolio injection)
- Full Gamma/CLOB/Data API coverage: events, outcomes, search, orderbooks, prices, price history, batch orderbooks, midpoints, spreads, trades, market parameters, open interest, live volume, user positions, user activity, total value, top holders, comments, categories, and tags
- Cross-platform comparison: crossref Polymarket markets against Kalshi for price discrepancies
- All tools return structuredContent with dataSources, dataFreshness, and fetchedAt metadata

Try asking:
- "What are the top 5 prediction markets by volume right now?"
- "Search for markets related to the 2028 presidential election"
- "How efficient is the pricing on the Trump vs Biden market? Is there arbitrage?"
- "Analyze whale flow on the Fed rate decision event who are the top holders and what is their cost basis?"
- "Show me markets between 30-70% probability with high volume that might be mispriced"
- "Analyze my Polymarket positions which ones have poor exit liquidity?"
- "Compare the Polymarket odds vs Kalshi odds on the next Fed meeting outcome"
- "Find correlated markets where a move in one should predict a move in another"
- "What are the orderbook depth and spread for all outcomes on event slug 'democratic-presidential-nominee-2028'?"
- "Browse all markets in the Politics category sorted by recent activity"
- "Build a high-conviction workflow: find mispriced markets, check whale positioning, then verify liquidity"

Agent tips:
- Use search_markets or get_top_markets for discovery, then get_event_outcomes for detailed outcome data
- analyze_market_liquidity returns whale cost simulation (estimated slippage for large exits) which is critical before sizing positions
- For portfolio analysis, the platform auto-injects positions via contextRequirements: ["polymarket"] -- no wallet key needed
- place_polymarket_order returns an external_action_required status with a direct Polymarket link because their CLOB requires API key auth that cannot be delegated
- Composite intelligence tools (find_arbitrage_opportunities, build_high_conviction_workflow) chain multiple API calls internally and return pre-computed insights
- get_batch_orderbooks is the most efficient way to fetch depth for multiple outcomes at once

mcpTools.name: polymarket_crossref_kalshi
mcpTools.description:
[POLYMARKET SERVER] Search Kalshi for markets equivalent to a Polymarket market.

This tool belongs to the POLYMARKET MCP server. Use it when you have a Polymarket market 
and want to find the corresponding market on Kalshi for comparison.

⚠️ Call this tool with POLYMARKET's toolId, not Kalshi's.

USE THIS when you have a Polymarket market and want to find the same on Kalshi for:
  - Price comparison / arbitrage detection
  - Cross-platform probability analysis
  - Finding additional liquidity

WORKFLOW:
  1. You have a Polymarket market (from get_event_by_slug or search_markets)
  2. Call: polymarket_crossref_kalshi({ keywords: "tariffs revenue 2025" })
  3. Returns matching Kalshi markets with prices

EXAMPLE INPUT:
  { "keywords": "tariffs revenue 2025" }

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
  {
    "searchedFor": { "keywords": "tariffs revenue 2025", "polymarketSlug": null },
    "kalshiResults": [
      { 
        "title": "Will tariffs generate...", 
        "ticker": "KXTARIFFS", 
        "yesPrice": 31, 
        "matchScore": 0.67,
        "rules": "If tariffs generate more than X by Y, then..."
      }
    ],
    "hint": "Found 1 potential matches on Kalshi...",
    "fetchedAt": "2025-01-10T..."
  }

NEXT STEPS after finding match:
  - ⚠️ CRITICAL: Compare the 'rules' field with Polymarket rules to ensure YES/NO mean the same thing!
  - Use Kalshi MCP tools (get_event, get_market) with the 'ticker' for deeper analysis
  - Compare yesPrice: Kalshi uses cents (31 = 31%), Polymarket uses decimals (0.31 = 31%)

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Always compare resolution rules before calculating arbitrage! Markets may define YES/NO differently.

⚠️ API LIMITATION: Kalshi's API does NOT support server-side text search. This tool fetches recent markets and filters client-side. For best results:
  - Use specific keywords from the market title
  - Works best for popular/high-volume markets
  - May miss less popular markets not in the first batch

⚠️ Kalshi has NO sports markets. For sports, use Odds API instead.

mcpTools.name: get_top_holders
mcpTools.description:
Get the top holders (biggest positions) for a specific market. Shows who the whales are, their position sizes, and implied conviction. Essential for smart money analysis.

DEEP FETCHING (default=true): Polymarket API caps at 20 holders per call with NO pagination. To work around this, we query 10 minBalance thresholds [$1M, $100k, $10k, $5k, $2k, $1k, $500, $100, $10, $1] in paced batches and deduplicate results. This captures everything from ultra-whales ($1M+) down to small positions, returning 50-100+ unique holders instead of just 20.

Set deepFetch=false for faster but shallower results (20 per side max).

⏱️ PERFORMANCE: With deepFetch=true, this runs 10 holder-tier queries in paced batches and typically takes ~8-15s.
⚠️ Call ALONE (not in parallel with other heavy tools) when deepFetch=true to avoid timeouts.

limit.description: Number of top holders to return per outcome (default: 50 with deepFetch, 20 without)

outcome.description: Which outcome to show holders for (default: BOTH)

deepFetch.description: Use multi-tier fetching to get more holders (default: true). Set to false for faster but limited results.

rateLimit.notes: Heavy Polymarket workflow. Call this tool alone and prefer narrower scopes first.

edgeCaseNotes: ⚠️ Call ALONE (not in parallel with other heavy tools) when deepFetch=true to avoid timeouts

tools.name: Coinglass
tools.description:
Crypto derivatives and on-chain intelligence from Coinglass covering open interest, funding rates, exchange flows, BTC valuation models, and market sentiment across 20+ exchanges.

Features:
- 7 intelligence tools: market sentiment, BTC valuation score, market regime, OI divergence scanner, exchange balance pressure, macro overview, and batch OI
- 20 raw data tools: funding rates, open interest by exchange, Fear & Greed history, AHR999, Rainbow Chart, Bubble Index, Puell Multiple, Stock-to-Flow, Bull Market Peak indicators, Bitcoin ETF flows/list, exchange balances with historical charts, futures pairs/markets, liquidation metadata, and spot coin support
- Batch OI endpoint fetches up to 15 coins in a single call, replacing per-coin loops
- Server-side rate limiter and TTL cache prevent upstream quota exhaustion
- Dynamic coin resolution when symbols are omitted (auto-selects highest-OI majors from live data)
- All responses normalized to snake_case with explicit data source and timestamp fields

Try asking:
- "What is the current market sentiment and Fear & Greed reading?"
- "Which coins and exchanges does Coinglass support for futures data?"
- "Compare open interest for BTC, ETH, SOL, and DOGE in one call"
- "Scan the top 20 coins for OI vs sentiment divergences with a 1% threshold"
- "Give me a full BTC valuation score, then check the market regime and ETF flows"
- "What is the exchange balance pressure for ETH right now, and is there net inflow or outflow?"
- "Get funding rates for BTC across all exchanges and show me the Rainbow Chart and Puell Multiple together"

Agent tips:
- Call get_supported_coins or get_supported_exchanges first to discover valid symbols and venues before querying
- Use get_oi_batch instead of calling get_oi_by_exchange in a loop to stay within rate limits
- analyze_market_sentiment, scan_oi_divergence, and get_oi_batch are bulk-first tools; prefer them over single-coin equivalents
- Tools publish _meta.rateLimit hints (maxRequestsPerMinute, cooldownMs, maxConcurrency) so planners can pace calls
- If a symbol parameter is omitted the server auto-resolves using live market data, so explicit symbols are optional for most tools

tools.name: Hyperliquid
tools.description:
The most comprehensive Hyperliquid MCP server: 48 tools spanning real-time market intelligence, portfolio analytics, perpetual and spot data, full order lifecycle management via EIP-712 handshake signing, TWAP execution, staking operations, and leverage/margin controls.

Features:
- 10 intelligence tools: price impact simulation, funding analysis, open interest heatmaps, large order absorption modeling, trader performance scoring, spot market analytics, whale wallet profiling, position risk analysis, vault shadow-exposure mapping, and full cross-source portfolio aggregation
- 23 raw data tools: orderbooks, candles, trades, funding history, exchange stats, volume history, spot metadata and balances, user state, open orders, order status, fills, fees, referral state, portfolio P&L, vault equities, sub-accounts, staking summaries, delegations, and OI-cap detection
- 13 write-action tools with EIP-712 handshake signing: place orders (limit, market, stop-loss, take-profit), cancel by oid or cloid, dead-man's switch, modify single or batch orders, update leverage and margin mode, adjust isolated margin, place and cancel TWAP orders, stake/unstake HYPE, and delegate/undelegate to validators
- Context injection support: tools that need user-specific data declare contextRequirements so the platform injects wallet and portfolio context automatically
- All tools return structuredContent with dataSources, dataFreshness, and fetchedAt for full auditability

Try asking:
- "What is the current ETH orderbook depth and liquidity score on Hyperliquid?"
- "List all available perpetual markets and their 24h volume"
- "Compare funding rates across BTC, ETH, and HYPE over the last week"
- "Simulate the price impact of selling 50 ETH right now"
- "Analyze the top whale wallet 0xabc... showing win rate, P&L, and fee efficiency"
- "Show my full portfolio exposure across direct positions, vaults, sub-accounts, and spot holdings"
- "Place a limit buy for 0.5 ETH at $3200 with post-only"
- "Cancel all my open HYPE orders and set a dead-man's switch for 10 minutes"
- "Set my BTC leverage to 10x isolated and add $500 of isolated margin"
- "Place a TWAP buy for 2 ETH over 30 minutes with randomized slicing"
- "Stake 100 HYPE and delegate it to validator 0x123..."
- "Which markets are currently at their open interest cap?"

Agent tips:
- Call list_markets first to discover available coins and their asset indices
- For write actions, the server returns a handshake_required status with an EIP-712 signature request; the platform handles the approval UI and calls submit_signed_action automatically
- Use closeEntirePosition=true on place_order to auto-calculate size from injected portfolio context
- Intelligence tools (prefixed analyze_ or calculate_) combine multiple API calls and return composite insights; raw data tools return normalized single-source responses
- All write tools are execute-only (surface: execute, queryEligible: false) and priced for execute mode
- For batch operations, batch_modify_orders supports up to 20 modifications per request

mcpTools.name: place_order
mcpTools.description: 🔐 WRITE ACTION: Place a perpetual order on Hyperliquid. Returns a signature request that must be approved by the user. Supports limit, market, stop-loss, and take-profit orders. The signature is used to authorize the order without exposing the user's private key. To CLOSE a position: set closeEntirePosition=true and the size will be auto-calculated from the user's current position.

size.description: Order size in base units (e.g., 0.1 ETH). NOT required if closeEntirePosition=true.

isBuy.description: true for long/buy, false for short/sell

price.description: Limit price. For market orders, this is auto-calculated with slippage.

postOnly.description: If true, order will only be placed if it would be a maker order (default: false)

portfolio.description: Optional: Your Hyperliquid portfolio context (injected by the Context app)

reduceOnly.description: If true, order can only reduce position size (default: false, auto-set true for SL/TP)

triggerPrice.description: Trigger price for stop-loss or take-profit orders (required for those types)

closeEntirePosition.description: If true, automatically use the FULL position size from portfolio context. Sets reduceOnly=true. Use this for closing positions instead of guessing the size.

rateLimit.notes: Write action. Use sparingly and only after explicit user intent.
